message:
chore(ft-xbnl0.2.4): add per-run + aggregate test counts to check script (tick 369)

Extended `scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh` to show test counts in the
per-run `[PASS]`/`[FAIL]` lines and in the final summary. Previously
the output was PASS/FAIL only; now it's:

  [PASS] Run 1/5: HTTP client contract tests — 24 tests
  [PASS] Run 2/5: TLS tests (...) — 45 tests
  [PASS] Run 3/5: Regression guards — 3 tests
  [PASS] Run 4/5: Metrics server cx-first family — 3 tests
  [PASS] Run 5/5: Web server cx pre-cancel — 1 tests
    ft-xbnl0.2.4 — all 5 runs PASS (76 tests)

Implementation:
- Added `TOTAL_PASSED` counter alongside the existing `FAIL` flag.
- `run_test` tees cargo's stdout to a tempfile, then greps for
  `test result: ok. N passed` and sums all N values (handles
  --all-targets splits that could produce multiple result lines
  per run).
- Final summary echoes the aggregate count on success, or "N passed
  before failure" on a failing run.

Why this helps the closer:
- Making the test count visible per-run means a partial regression
  (e.g. "Run 1 dropped from 24 to 21 tests") surfaces without having
  to diff the full cargo-test output.
- The aggregate count is the closure-checklist gate (currently "76
  tests"); matching it against the output line is trivial for a
  human or CI summarizer.

No test code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,7 @@ export CARGO_TARGET_DIR
 export CC CXX
 
 FAIL=0
+TOTAL_PASSED=0
 
 log_header() {
     echo
@@ -60,12 +61,24 @@ run_test() {
     local label="$1"
     shift
     log_header "${label}"
-    if cargo test "$@"; then
-        echo "[PASS] ${label}"
+    # Capture stdout + stderr to a tempfile so we can tally the "N passed"
+    # count while still showing the output to the operator.
+    local tmp
+    tmp="$(mktemp -t ft-xbnl0-2-4-check.XXXXXX)"
+    if cargo test "$@" 2>&1 | tee "${tmp}"; then
+        # Sum all "N passed" counts from all `test result: ok.` lines in
+        # this run (per-binary splits may produce multiple result lines
+        # under --all-targets; normally there's just one for our filters).
+        local passed
+        passed="$(grep -oE 'test result: ok\. [0-9]+ passed' "${tmp}" \
+            | awk '{ s += $4 } END { print (s ? s : 0) }')"
+        TOTAL_PASSED=$(( TOTAL_PASSED + passed ))
+        echo "[PASS] ${label} — ${passed} tests"
     else
         echo "[FAIL] ${label}"
         FAIL=1
     fi
+    rm -f "${tmp}"
 }
 
 run_test "Run 1/5: HTTP client contract tests" \
@@ -101,11 +114,11 @@ run_test "Run 5/5: Web server cx pre-cancel" \
 echo
 echo "=============================================================="
 if [[ ${FAIL} -eq 0 ]]; then
-    echo "  ft-xbnl0.2.4 — all 5 runs PASS"
+    echo "  ft-xbnl0.2.4 — all 5 runs PASS (${TOTAL_PASSED} tests)"
     echo "=============================================================="
     exit 0
 else
-    echo "  ft-xbnl0.2.4 — ONE OR MORE RUNS FAILED"
+    echo "  ft-xbnl0.2.4 — ONE OR MORE RUNS FAILED (${TOTAL_PASSED} tests passed before failure)"
     echo "=============================================================="
     exit 1
 fi
